status_notes: Initial research from GCP Cloud Storage documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs) and partial Pass 2 (API). Needs Pass 3 (cross-reference) and Pass 5 (human review with live GCP environment).
notes: Object storage for GCP. Security includes encryption, IAM, uniform bucket-level access, object versioning, retention policies, VPC Service Controls.

match_nodes.description: Encryption at rest for Cloud Storage objects

data.description: All data stored in Cloud Storage is encrypted at rest by default. Three key management options available.

options.name: Google-managed encryption keys (GMEK)
options.description: Default. Google manages keys with automatic rotation. No configuration required.

options.name: Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK)
options.description: Keys managed in Cloud KMS. Customer controls key rotation, access, and lifecycle.

options.name: Customer-supplied encryption keys (CSEK)
options.description: Customer provides encryption key with each request. Google does not store the key. Key loss means data loss.

default.note: All data encrypted by default with Google-managed keys. No opt-out possible.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use CMEK with Cloud KMS for regulatory requirements or key lifecycle control. GMEK is sufficient for most workloads.

configure.note: CSEK must be provided per-request via x-goog-encryption-key header.

match_nodes.description: Uniform bucket-level access disabling ACLs in favor of IAM-only

data.description: Disables object ACLs and requires all access to be managed through IAM policies only. Simplifies access management and provides consistent permissions across all objects in a bucket. Once enabled for 90 consecutive days, it becomes permanent and cannot be reverted.

default.note: Uniform bucket-level access is enabled by default for new buckets.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable uniform bucket-level access. Manage all access through IAM instead of ACLs.

match_nodes.description: Prevent public access at bucket or organization level

data.description: Prevents data in Cloud Storage from being exposed to the public internet. Can be set at bucket level or enforced via organization policy across all buckets.

options.description: Public access is blocked. Existing public permissions become ineffective.
Public access prevention is inherited from organization policy. If no org policy, public access is allowed.

data.vendor_recommendation: Set public access prevention to 'enforced' for all buckets containing non-public data. Use organization policy to enforce across all projects.

configure.note: Organization-level enforcement via organization policy constraint storage.publicAccessPrevention

match_nodes.description: Bucket-level IAM policies for access control

data.description: IAM policies on buckets define who has what access. When uniform bucket-level access is enabled, IAM is the sole mechanism for controlling access to buckets and objects.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use IAM policies with the principle of least privilege. Prefer predefined roles (Storage Object Viewer, Storage Object Creator, Storage Object Admin) over primitive roles (Owner, Editor, Viewer).

match_nodes.description: Maintain previous versions of objects in Cloud Storage

data.description: Retains a noncurrent version of an object when it is replaced or deleted. Protects against accidental deletion and overwrites. Noncurrent versions can be listed, accessed, and restored.

default.note: Object versioning is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable versioning for buckets containing important data. Use lifecycle rules to manage noncurrent version retention and control storage costs.

match_nodes.description: Minimum retention period preventing object deletion

data.description: Sets a minimum retention period for objects in a bucket. Objects cannot be deleted or replaced until the retention period expires. Can be locked to make the policy permanent (irreversible). Supports regulatory compliance (SEC 17a-4, CFTC 1.31).
data.vendor_recommendation: Use retention policies for compliance requirements. Lock the policy only when permanent retention enforcement is required — locked policies cannot be removed or shortened.

configure.note: Locking a retention policy is irreversible. The bucket cannot be deleted until all objects meet the retention period.

match_nodes.description: Prevent object deletion using event-based or temporary holds

data.description: Holds prevent individual objects from being deleted or replaced. Two types available, independent of retention policies.

options.description: Applied by default to new objects (if configured on bucket). Released when event-based hold is removed. Starts retention period on release.
Applied to specific objects. Must be explicitly removed before object can be deleted.

match_nodes.description: Automatic object management rules for storage class transition and deletion

data.description: Rules that automatically manage objects based on conditions like age, storage class, version count. Security-relevant uses: delete noncurrent versions after a period, clean up incomplete uploads, enforce data retention.
data.vendor_recommendation: Configure lifecycle rules to delete noncurrent versions after a defined period, and transition infrequently accessed data to cheaper storage classes.

match_nodes.description: Usage and storage logs for Cloud Storage buckets

data.description: Records detailed information about requests made to a bucket. Usage logs contain request details (requester, operation, object). Storage logs contain storage consumption data. Logs are delivered to a destination bucket as CSV files.

default.note: Access logging is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable access logging for security auditing. Consider Cloud Audit Logs as the primary audit mechanism — access logs are supplementary.

match_nodes.description: Restrict Cloud Storage data exfiltration using VPC Service Controls perimeters

data.description: VPC Service Controls create security perimeters around Cloud Storage buckets to prevent data exfiltration. Requests to Cloud Storage from outside the perimeter are blocked. Provides defense-in-depth beyond IAM policies.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use VPC Service Controls for buckets containing highly sensitive data. Define service perimeters that include Cloud Storage and related services (BigQuery, Dataflow) to prevent data from leaving the perimeter.
